mainEntity.name: What is a power factor capacitor bank?

acceptedAnswer.text: A power factor capacitor bank is a set of capacitors connected in parallel to your electrical supply to provide reactive power locally. It improves your power factor (PF) from a typical 0.70–0.80 to 0.92–0.98, reducing NEPRA reactive-power surcharges, cutting cable losses, and freeing transformer capacity.

mainEntity.name: How much does a capacitor bank cost in Pakistan in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: A fixed 10 kVAR capacitor bank costs PKR 14,000–25,000. A 50 kVAR APFC panel (automatic, 6-step) costs PKR 120,000–220,000. Prices depend on IEC certification level, housing material, detuning reactor inclusion, and origin tier.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate the kVAR size I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the formula: Qc (kVAR) = P (kW) × (tan φ1 − tan φ2). For a 100 kW factory at 0.75 PF targeting 0.95 PF, you need approximately 55 kVAR. For variable loads, choose an APFC panel with auto-switching steps.

mainEntity.name: What is an APFC panel and do I need one?

acceptedAnswer.text: An Automatic Power Factor Controller (APFC) panel uses a PF relay to switch capacitor steps in and out automatically as your load varies. Most industrial consumers above 50 kW benefit from APFC over fixed banks.

mainEntity.name: What happens if my power factor stays below 0.85 on my NEPRA bill?

acceptedAnswer.text: NEPRA levies a reactive-power surcharge (typically 2–5 PKR/kVArh excess) on industrial consumers falling below 0.85 monthly average PF. For a 200 kW factory, annual penalties can exceed PKR 300,000–400,000.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a detuning reactor with my capacitor bank?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if your load includes VFDs, welding sets, arc furnaces, UPS systems, or any switched-mode power supply. A 7% detuned reactor (189 Hz tuning) is mandatory for modern industrial facilities in Pakistan.

mainEntity.name: What is the lifespan of a capacitor bank in Pakistani conditions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Economy-grade capacitors last 4–7 years in Pakistan's hot climate. IEC 60831-certified MKP polypropylene units last 10–12 years. Premium European-specification units achieve 15+ years.
